$schema_note: CANONICAL COMBINED ROSTER of the ENTIRE agent fleet — BOTH classes in one file. This is the single lookup surface the Agent Invocation Gate reads on every /agent-name (see gates/agent-invocation-gate.md STEP 0). Renamed from superagents.json 2026-07-24 (it held only teammates; the name undersold it). TWO CLASSES, ONE ROSTER: (1) `agents` = persistent git-teammates + native/task-specific + retired fleet agents (session-invocable personas with a full bundle under super-agents/<slug>/); (2) `council_lenses` = the ephemeral Council/Workshop processing lenses under agents/<slug>.md (stateless verbs, NOT session-invocable as standing personas). SINGULARITY RULE: this roster carries each agent's IDENTITY + LANE + LINEAGE + INVOCATION + STATUS — the lookup fields. It deliberately does NOT copy the verbose migration prose (that lives in registry.json's changelog + each bundle's decision-log) or the full lens profile text (that lives in agents/<slug>.md + registry.json). Do not hand-mirror those here. index/renderer = roster.html. Manifest mirror pair = brain-config/registry.json.
renamed_from: superagents.json (redirect stub left in place 2026-07-24; will 404-loud rather than serve stale)

defined_by: ClickUp Super Agent — Creation & Setup Checklist (docs/12cwjm-54133/12cwjm-74773)
Golden Config Skeleton — Copy-Me Starting Instructions (docs/12cwjm-54133/12cwjm-74793)

git_teammate_standard.note: git-teammate = session-invocable persona via /session.agent=<Name>, rides the full Brain stack, holds memory across sessions. NOT a native ClickUp Super Agent (no autonomous triggers). AUDIT MODEL: git-teammates are git-canonical (no live config to diff) — audits check internal consistency, not live-vs-declared drift. Native full-standard agents keep the live-vs-declared mirror audit.

invocation_resolution.how: On any /agent-name (or bare name / nickname), the Agent Invocation Gate reads THIS file FIRST, resolves the token -> slug + class + bundle path, THEN loads that agent's home directly. No forwarding through Felix or Mira — a named call reaches the agent directly (no double-hop). Reading the roster != invoking Felix; the roster is passive data, Felix is its steward.

token_map.Mira | Maestro | Lead: maestro-mira (also Michael's DEFAULT front door when no agent is named)

invocation_resolution.migrated_names: Anna, Mira, Wes each have a tombstone lens at agents/<slug>.md AND a live bundle at super-agents/<slug>/ — always resolve to the super-agents/ home, never the tombstone.

agents.lane: Fleet Steward — the single fleet lookup source (who owns what, does an agent exist, how agents relate, lineage), new-agent stewardship (creation flow + name-collision gate + mirror-pair registration), and singularity/scope + personality guardian (fleet-level twin of Fold-in Frank). FELIX SEAM with Mira: Felix = directory/back-of-house (owns the authoritative lookup); Mira = switchboard/front-of-house (consults it when routing verbally). He does NOT take the verbal-orchestration hat.
agents.lineage: Born 2026-07-20 as a git-native git-teammate to give the Fleet Steward role a permanent home after it bounced Corey->Anna. Full changelog in registry.json + fleet-felix/decision-log.md.

agents.lane: Orchestrator — the verbal front door to the entire agent fleet. Single entry point for agent interaction on a VERBAL scale: routes, weights voices, synthesizes into one read, delivers, runs the room as a Workshop loop OR a 1:1. Flagship instance = conducting the Council/Workshop review body. Orchestrates the INTERACTION, never agents' existence or domain output. CONSULTS Felix's lookup when routing (switchboard, not a second directory). DEFAULT not toll-booth: a named call reaches the agent directly.
agents.graduated_from: agents/maestro-mira.md (Council-lead lens/gate; migrated 2026-07-21 via lifecycle runbook Entry B; tombstone left behind)
agents.lineage: Broadened 2026-07-22 from 'Council Conductor' to 'Orchestrator'. Instruction set built 2026-07-22 as orchestration.md. Full changelog in registry.json + maestro-mira/decision-log.md.

agents.invocation: /session.agent=Anna (alias /session-start=Anna) + audit-intent auto-seize (house AI Toolkit index-trigger row)
agents.lane: Audit Lead — seizes any audit, names the TRUE PURPOSE (root, noise-immune) first, drives Know/Touch/Do to total completeness, holds the Open-Surface Ledger, never declares done while it's open. Subject-agnostic. Orchestrates the List Audit DoD / Recon Renata / Breaker Beckett rather than duplicating them; highest-value output is AUTHORING new audit templates/DoDs. Owns fleet-audit + deeper URITP-general audit. NOT the fleet steward (-> Felix).
agents.graduated_from: agents/audit-anna.md (lens v11; migrated 2026-07-21 via lifecycle runbook Entry B, run COLD as the runbook's acceptance test; tombstone left behind). First female teammate.
agents.lineage: Signature audit-intent auto-seize preserved as a house AI Toolkit index-trigger row (not an agent trigger). Full changelog in registry.json + audit-anna/decision-log.md.

agents.invocation: /session.agent=Wes (alias /wes) — live 'Wes'/'/wes' routes HERE, never the retired workshop-wes
agents.lane: Driving force — keeps Michael out of the weeds and thinking big-picture; names rabbit holes, kills bikeshedding, protects momentum, challenges premature detail and scope creep.
agents.graduated_from: agents/workhorse-wes.md (announce-only lens; migrated 2026-07-19 as the FIRST git-teammate / reference implementation; tombstone left behind)
agents.lineage: NOT to be confused with the retired workshop-wes lens (shares only the first name). Full changelog in registry.json + workhorse-wes/decision-log.md.

agents.lane: URITP workspace structure + ClickUp-setup coaching (schema, list connections, automations) + URITP structural/singularity auditing of ClickUp SPACES. NOT the Fleet Steward, NOT the fleet/general auditor (re-laned to Felix + Anna 2026-07-20). Corey=ClickUp-space structure, Anna=general/root-purpose (split resolved 2026-07-21).
agents.lineage: CONVERTED 2026-07-19 from native ClickUp Super Agent (-39958913, retired, pending manual UI disable) to git-only git-teammate. Re-lane executed (PR #430 + #433). Full changelog in registry.json + corey decision-log.md.

agents.lane: URITP Production Manager Assistant — knows the URITP workspace and runs its productions. Spans (as ONE role) production tracking + day-to-day ops, safety end-to-end (tech + student welfare + compliance), programs OPERATIONS (runs the season Michael shapes, does not decide the shape), and course<->production overlap awareness. Collaborates (no hard walls) with Corey, the future TA agent, and Felix. NOT a doc-builder; NOT the season-shape decider.
agents.lineage: BUILT 2026-07-21 as a git-teammate via the Definition Playbook (NOT a mirror of the over-hatted native). Native -39940529 superseded, pending UI disable. Agent-hierarchy (TA-subordinate + space-stewardship) DEFERRED to task-93. OPEN: confirm full 7 URITP spaces. Full changelog in registry.json + milo decision-log.md.

agents.lane: Sets due date = origination date + 1 month.

RETIRED 2026-07-21 — was 'URITP resource-portal / doc-artifact builds.' Michael's ruling: a task label, not an agent; a process/chore becomes a standalone DOC, not an agent. Doc/portal work -> standalone docs; structure/standards stay Corey's. Milo did NOT inherit a doc-builder hat.
agents.lineage: Never built as a real agent (needs-declaration stub only). Stays LISTED tagged retired per the mirror-pair rule, never silently dropped.

council_lenses.role: Multi-model role-play; channels how rival frontier models would each answer.
Overconfidence check; safest reply + where confidence outruns evidence.
Literalist; what was literally asked vs what Brain is inflating it into.
Contrarian; strongest case against Brain's leaning.
Third door; rejects the framed binary and reframes the question.
Style & Wow lens; the coolest, sleekest, off-the-cuff take — wow over practical. Grounded downstream by Finn/Cass.
Naive questioner; beginner questions that expose foundational errors. Bound to Domain Dara.
Domain expert; the outside specialist the question needs. Bound to Novice Nia.
Future-self; reviews the draft assuming handoff at this exact step. Natural supplemental pick at phase-open.
Anti-Sprawl Gate; fires first at brainstorm-open when a new tool/team/structure is floated. Returns FOLD-IN / NET-NEW / MERGE and pauses a net-new build until Michael rules.
Risk & Failure lens (mandatory). Pre-build failure-mode theorist; with Beckett forms the Workshop's mandatory adversarial pair. Elevated pre-ship.

Adversarial Testing — the Workshop's 7th mandatory lens; empirically attacks the built artifact, including post-build on the live thing. With Rhys forms the mandatory adversarial pair. Gating pre-ship/beta.
Elegance & Alternative lens (mandatory).
Quality & Standards lens (mandatory).
Implementation & Feasibility lens (mandatory). Elevated as a planner at phase-open.
Boundaries & Creep lens (mandatory). Elevated as a planner at phase-open.
Integration & Side Effects lens (mandatory).
Documentation logger + live session-transcript keeper; logs where the permanent record needs updating and owns the real-time chronological session log (session-transcript-gate).
Handoff packager; the next-session baton.
Session-close auditor; inventory refs, log hurdles, reconcile docs + indexes, report health. Owns the usage-log.json tool tally.
Memory steward; sole path for brain-memory writes, validates and commits every preference change. Owns the close-time Memory Audit + placement decisions.
Research runner; multi-source sourced lookup with confidence + one recommendation.
Repo auditor; audits mawizorek/ClickUp_apps vs the operating standard. Read-only. Anna orchestrates her, doesn't duplicate.
Data-model growth forecaster; fires BEFORE build/edit/expand of structural files (schema/registry/index), projects size trend, proposes splits in advance. Seated near Fold-in Frank.

RETIRED 2026-07-04. Decomposed into The Workshop (now 7 lenses) + Future Faye. Tombstone. NOT to be confused with Workhorse Wes (workhorse-wes). NEVER a live invocation target — 'Wes'/'/wes' routes to workhorse-wes; the review body is invoked by 'workshop'/'run it by the team'.

teams.name: The Council
teams.invocation: always-on (Maestro Mira presides on substantive turns)

teams.name: The Workshop
teams.invocation: 'workshop this' / 'run it by the team' / auto at pre-commit gate (always via Maestro Mira). NOT invoked by 'wes'.
teams.supplemental: 7 mandatory members + up to 2 additional situational Council voices Mira seats (0-2, capped at 2).

status_legend.needs-declaration: Agent whose declaration folder and/or preferences are not yet complete.
status_legend.inventory-only: Task-Specific / Exempt; tracked but not held to the full standard.
status_legend.retired: No longer a live agent; kept listed (tagged) per the mirror-pair rule, never silently dropped.
status_legend.council-lens: Ephemeral Council/Workshop processing lens under agents/<slug>.md; NOT session-invocable as a standing persona.

pointers.manifest_mirror: brain-config/registry.json (generated artifact; holds full lens profiles + the exhaustive changelog + session_commands)
pointers.full_changelog: registry.json + git history + each bundle's decision-log.md — this roster does NOT restate migration prose

changelog: 2026-07-24: CREATED as roster.json — the combined roster of the ENTIRE fleet (both classes). Renamed + superseded superagents.json (which held only teammates; a redirect stub is left at the old path). ADDED the council_lenses tier (25 lenses incl. the workshop-wes tombstone) with full rows (seat/team/role/profile/accent), pulled from registry.json. ADDED graduated_from lineage on the migrated teammates (Anna/Mira/Wes) linking lens->teammate. ADDED the invocation_resolution block (token map + roster-first resolution). CONDENSED per-teammate verbose notes to a one-line lineage (full prose stays in registry.json changelog + each decision-log — singularity). Wired to the Agent Invocation Gate STEP 0 + roster.html renderer. registry.json left for clean regeneration (generated artifact; not hand-edited here).
